# The Pro Learning Framework

Learning techniques based on how the brain actually works.

**Case: The Pomodoro Technique. 25 minutes of focused work, 5 minute break. Trains the brain to focus.**

**Case: Active Recall. Testing yourself is far more effective than re-reading notes.**

**Case: Spaced Repetition. Review material at increasing intervals to build long-term memory.**

**Case: Chunking. Group information into meaningful wholes. Experts chunk automatically.**

**Case: Interleaving. Mix different problem types. Harder in the moment, better long-term.**

**Case: The Diffuse Mode. The relaxed, big-picture mode of thinking. Essential for creativity.**
**Case: The Focused Mode. Concentrated attention on a specific task. Essential for learning details.**
**Case: The Memory Palace. Ancient technique for remembering lists by associating them with locations.**
**Case: The Feynman Technique. Explain the concept in simple terms. If you cannot explain it simply, you do not understand it.**
**Case: The Practice. Deliberate practice, not just repetition, is what builds expertise.**
**Case: The Feedback. Getting immediate feedback on your performance accelerates learning.**
**Case: The Difficulty. Desirable difficulty — learning that is hard enough to challenge but not so hard it frustrates.**
